growth_records.medium: Defined marine anaerobic medium (BS-type) with glucose as carbon source
growth_records.cite: Subhash Yadav et al. Organic matter degradation in the deep, sulfidic waters of the Black Sea: insights into the ecophysiology of novel anaerobic bacteria. Microbiome (2024). DOI:10.1186/s40168-024-01816-x. [PMC11129491]
growth_records.snippet: Oceanispirochaeta sp. strains M1(T) and M2 exhibited fast growth on simple molecules like glucose (doubling time 2-3 h) and pyruvate (with a doubling time; 1.5-2 h; Fig. S17, S18)
growth_records.notes: Doubling time 2-3 h on glucose (Fig. S17/S18). Midpoint 2.5 h; mu = 0.693/2.5 = 0.277 h-1 (range 0.231-0.347). Strain M2 reported jointly. Carbon source assumed ~2 g/L per substitution protocol.

growth_records.medium: Defined marine anaerobic medium (BS-type) with pyruvate as carbon source
growth_records.cite: Subhash Yadav et al. Organic matter degradation in the deep, sulfidic waters of the Black Sea: insights into the ecophysiology of novel anaerobic bacteria. Microbiome (2024). DOI:10.1186/s40168-024-01816-x. [PMC11129491]
growth_records.snippet: fast growth on simple molecules like glucose (doubling time 2-3 h) and pyruvate (with a doubling time; 1.5-2 h; Fig. S17, S18)
growth_records.notes: Doubling time 1.5-2 h on pyruvate. Midpoint 1.75 h; mu = 0.693/1.75 = 0.396 h-1 (range 0.347-0.462).
